NKJV.1: Now after six days Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, led them up on a high mountain by themselves;
NKJV.2: and He was transfigured before them. His face shone like the sun, and His clothes became as white as the light.
NKJV.3: And behold, Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Him.
NKJV.4: Then Peter answered and said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here; if You wish, let us make here three tabernacles: one for You, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
NKJV.5: While he was still speaking, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them; and suddenly a voice came out of the cloud, saying, “This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. Hear Him!”
NKJV.6: And when the disciples heard it, they fell on their faces and were greatly afraid.
NKJV.7: But Jesus came and touched them and said, “Arise, and do not be afraid.”
NKJV.8: When they had lifted up their eyes, they saw no one but Jesus only.
NKJV.9: Now as they came down from the mountain, Jesus commanded them, saying, “Tell the vision to no one until the Son of Man is risen from the dead.”
NKJV.10: And His disciples asked Him, saying, “Why then do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”
NKJV.11: Jesus answered and said to them, “Indeed, Elijah is coming first and will  restore all things.
NKJV.12: But I say to you that Elijah has come already, and they  did not know him but did to him whatever they wished. Likewise  the Son of Man is also about to suffer at their hands.”
NKJV.13: Then the disciples understood that He spoke to them of John the Baptist.
NKJV.14: And when they had come to the multitude, a man came to Him, kneeling down to Him and saying,
NKJV.15: “Lord, have mercy on my son, for he is an epileptic and suffers severely; for he often falls into the fire and often into the water.
NKJV.16: So I brought him to Your disciples, but they could not cure him.”
NKJV.17: Then Jesus answered and said, “O faithless and  perverse generation, how long shall I be with you? How long shall I bear with you? Bring him here to Me.”
NKJV.18: And Jesus rebuked the demon, and it came out of him; and the child was cured from that very hour.
NKJV.19: Then the disciples came to Jesus privately and said, “Why could we not cast it out?”
NKJV.20: So Jesus said to them, “Because of your unbelief; for assuredly, I say to you,  if you have faith as a mustard seed, you will say to this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move; and nothing will be impossible for you.
NKJV.21: However, this kind does not go out except by prayer and fasting.”
NKJV.22: Now while they were staying in Galilee, Jesus said to them, “The Son of Man is about to be betrayed into the hands of men,
NKJV.23: and they will kill Him, and the third day He will be raised up.” And they were exceedingly sorrowful.
NKJV.24: When they had come to Capernaum, those who received the temple tax came to Peter and said, “Does your Teacher not pay the temple tax?”
NKJV.25: He said, “Yes.” And when he had come into the house, Jesus anticipated him, saying, “What do you think, Simon? From whom do the kings of the earth take customs or taxes, from their sons or from  strangers?”
NKJV.26: Peter said to Him, “From strangers.” Jesus said to him, “Then the sons are free.
NKJV.27: Nevertheless, lest we offend them, go to the sea, cast in a hook, and take the fish that comes up first. And when you have opened its mouth, you will find a piece of money; take that and give it to them for Me and you.”

ESV.1: And after six days Jesus took with him Peter and James, and John his brother, and led them up a high mountain by themselves.
ESV.2: And he was transfigured before them, and his face shone like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
ESV.3: And behold, there appeared to them Moses and Elijah, talking with him.
ESV.4: And Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good that we are here. If you wish, I will make three tents here, one for you and one for Moses and one for Elijah.”
ESV.5: He was still speaking when, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them, and a voice from the cloud said, “This is my beloved Son,  with whom I am well pleased; listen to him.”
ESV.6: When the disciples heard this, they fell on their faces and were terrified.
ESV.7: But Jesus came and touched them, saying, “Rise, and have no fear.”
ESV.8: And when they lifted up their eyes, they saw no one but Jesus only.
ESV.9: And as they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them,  “Tell no one the vision, until the Son of Man is raised from the dead.”
ESV.10: And the disciples asked him, “Then why do the scribes say that first Elijah must come?”
ESV.11: He answered, “Elijah does come, and he will restore all things.
ESV.12: But I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they did not recognize him, but did to him whatever they pleased. So also the Son of Man will certainly suffer at their hands.”
ESV.13: Then the disciples understood that he was speaking to them of John the Baptist.
ESV.14: And when they came to the crowd, a man came up to him and, kneeling before him,
ESV.15: said, “Lord, have mercy on my son, for he has seizures and he suffers terribly. For often he falls into the fire, and often into the water.
ESV.16: And I brought him to your disciples, and they could not heal him.”
ESV.17: And Jesus answered, “O faithless and twisted generation, how long am I to be with you? How long am I to bear with you? Bring him here to me.”
ESV.18: And Jesus rebuked the demon,  and it  came out of him, and the boy was healed instantly.
ESV.19: Then the disciples came to Jesus privately and said, “Why could we not cast it out?”
ESV.20: He said to them,  “Because of your little faith. For truly, I say to you, if you have faith like a grain of mustard seed, you will say to this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move, and nothing will be impossible for you.”
ESV.22: As they were gathering  in Galilee, Jesus said to them, “The Son of Man is about to be delivered into the hands of men,
ESV.23: and they will kill him, and he will be raised on the third day.” And they were greatly distressed.
ESV.24: When they came to Capernaum, the collectors of the two-drachma tax went up to Peter and said, “Does your teacher not pay the tax?”
ESV.25: He said, “Yes.” And when he came into the house, Jesus spoke to him first, saying,  “What do you think, Simon? From whom do kings of the earth take toll or tax? From their sons or from others?”
ESV.26: And when he said, “From others,” Jesus said to him, “Then the sons are free.
ESV.27: However, not to give offense to them, go to the sea and cast a hook and take the first fish that comes up, and when you open its mouth you will find a shekel.  Take that and give it to them for me and for yourself.”

NASB.1: Six days later Jesus took with Him Peter and James and John his brother, and led them up on a high mountain by themselves.
NASB.2: And He was transfigured before them; and His face shone like the sun, and His garments became as white as light.
NASB.3: And behold, Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Him.
NASB.4: Peter said to Jesus, 'Lord, it is good for us to be here; if You wish, I will make three tabernacles here, one for You, and one for Moses, and one for Elijah.'
NASB.5: While he was still speaking, a bright cloud overshadowed them, and behold, a voice out of the cloud said, 'This is My beloved Son, with whom I am well-pleased; listen to Him!'
NASB.6: When the disciples heard [this], they fell face down to the ground and were terrified.
NASB.7: And Jesus came to [them] and touched them and said, 'Get up, and do not be afraid.'
NASB.8: And lifting up their eyes, they saw no one except Jesus Himself alone.
NASB.9: As they were coming down from the mountain, Jesus commanded them, saying, 'Tell the vision to no one until the Son of Man has risen from the dead.'
NASB.10: And His disciples asked Him, 'Why then do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?'
NASB.11: And He answered and said, 'Elijah is coming and will restore all things;
NASB.12: but I say to you that Elijah already came, and they did not recognize him, but did to him whatever they wished. So also the Son of Man is going to suffer at their hands.'
NASB.13: Then the disciples understood that He had spoken to them about John the Baptist.
NASB.14: When they came to the crowd, a man came up to Jesus, falling on his knees before Him and saying,
NASB.15: 'Lord, have mercy on my son, for he is a lunatic and is very ill; for he often falls into the fire and often into the water.
NASB.16: 'I brought him to Your disciples, and they could not cure him.'
NASB.17: And Jesus answered and said, 'You unbelieving and perverted generation, how long shall I be with you? How long shall I put up with you? Bring him here to Me.'
NASB.18: And Jesus rebuked him, and the demon came out of him, and the boy was cured at once.
NASB.19: Then the disciples came to Jesus privately and said, 'Why could we not drive it out?'
NASB.20: And He said to them, 'Because of the littleness of your faith; for truly I say to you, if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you will say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move; and nothing will be impossible to you.
NASB.21: ['But this kind does not go out except by prayer and fasting.]'
NASB.22: And while they were gathering together in Galilee, Jesus said to them, 'The Son of Man is going to be delivered into the hands of men;
NASB.23: and they will kill Him, and He will be raised on the third day.' And they were deeply grieved.
NASB.24: When they came to Capernaum, those who collected the two-drachma [tax] came to Peter and said, 'Does your teacher not pay the two-drachma [tax]?'
NASB.25: He said, 'Yes.' And when he came into the house, Jesus spoke to him first, saying, 'What do you think, Simon? From whom do the kings of the earth collect customs or poll-tax, from their sons or from strangers?'
NASB.26: When Peter said, 'From strangers,' Jesus said to him, 'Then the sons are exempt.
NASB.27: 'However, so that we do not offend them, go to the sea and throw in a hook, and take the first fish that comes up; and when you open its mouth, you will find a shekel. Take that and give it to them for you and Me.'

NLT.1: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the two brothers, James and John, and led them up a high mountain to be alone.
NLT.2: As the men watched, Jesus’ appearance was transformed so that his face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
NLT.3: Suddenly, Moses and Elijah appeared and began talking with Jesus.
NLT.4: Peter exclaimed, “Lord, it’s wonderful for us to be here! If you want, I’ll make three shelters as memorials — one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
NLT.5: But even as he spoke, a bright cloud overshadowed them, and a voice from the cloud said, “This is my dearly loved Son, who brings me great joy. Listen to him.”
NLT.6: The disciples were terrified and fell face down on the ground.
NLT.7: Then Jesus came over and touched them. “Get up,” he said. “Don’t be afraid.”
NLT.8: And when they looked up, Moses and Elijah were gone, and they saw only Jesus.
NLT.9: As they went back down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, “Don’t tell anyone what you have seen until the Son of Man has been raised from the dead.”
NLT.10: Then his disciples asked him, “Why do the teachers of religious law insist that Elijah must return before the Messiah comes? ”
NLT.11: Jesus replied, “Elijah is indeed coming first to get everything ready.
NLT.12: But I tell you, Elijah has already come, but he wasn’t recognized, and they chose to abuse him. And in the same way they will also make the Son of Man suffer.”
NLT.13: Then the disciples realized he was talking about John the Baptist.
NLT.14: At the foot of the mountain, a large crowd was waiting for them. A man came and knelt before Jesus and said,
NLT.15: “Lord, have mercy on my son. He has seizures and suffers terribly. He often falls into the fire or into the water.
NLT.16: So I brought him to your disciples, but they couldn’t heal him.”
NLT.17: Jesus said, “You faithless and corrupt people! How long must I be with you? How long must I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.”
NLT.18: Then Jesus rebuked the demon in the boy, and it left him. From that moment the boy was well.
NLT.19: Afterward the disciples asked Jesus privately, “Why couldn’t we cast out that demon?”
NLT.20: “You don’t have enough faith,” Jesus told them. “I tell you the truth, if you had faith even as small as a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it would move. Nothing would be impossible. ”
NLT.22: After they gathered again in Galilee, Jesus told them, “The Son of Man is going to be betrayed into the hands of his enemies.
NLT.23: He will be killed, but on the third day he will be raised from the dead.” And the disciples were filled with grief.
NLT.24: On their arrival in Capernaum, the collectors of the Temple tax came to Peter and asked him, “Doesn’t your teacher pay the Temple tax?”
NLT.25: “Yes, he does,” Peter replied. Then he went into the house. But before he had a chance to speak, Jesus asked him, “What do you think, Peter? Do kings tax their own people or the people they have conquered? ”
NLT.26: “They tax the people they have conquered,” Peter replied. “Well, then,” Jesus said, “the citizens are free!
NLT.27: However, we don’t want to offend them, so go down to the lake and throw in a line. Open the mouth of the first fish you catch, and you will find a large silver coin. Take it and pay the tax for both of us.”

WEB.1: After six days, Jesus took with him Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them up into a high mountain by themselves.
WEB.2: He was transfigured before them. His face shone like the sun, and his garments became as white as the light.
WEB.3: Behold, Moses and Elijah appeared to them talking with him.
WEB.4: Peter answered, and said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you want, let’s make three tents here: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
WEB.5: While he was still speaking, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them. Behold, a voice came out of the cloud, saying, “This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. Listen to him.”
WEB.6: When the disciples heard it, they fell on their faces, and were very afraid.
WEB.7: Jesus came and touched them and said, “Get up, and don’t be afraid.”
WEB.8: Lifting up their eyes, they saw no one, except Jesus alone.
WEB.9: As they were coming down from the mountain, Jesus commanded them, saying, “Don’t tell anyone what you saw, until the Son of Man has risen from the dead.”
WEB.10: His disciples asked him, saying, “Then why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”
WEB.11: Jesus answered them, “Elijah indeed comes first, and will restore all things,
WEB.12: but I tell you that Elijah has come already, and they didn’t recognize him, but did to him whatever they wanted to. Even so the Son of Man will also suffer by them.”
WEB.13: Then the disciples understood that he spoke to them of John the Baptizer.
WEB.14: When they came to the multitude, a man came to him, kneeling down to him, and saying,
WEB.15: “Lord, have mercy on my son, for he is epileptic, and suffers grievously; for he often falls into the fire, and often into the water.
WEB.16: So I brought him to your disciples, and they could not cure him.”
WEB.17: Jesus answered, “Faithless and perverse generation! How long will I be with you? How long will I bear with you? Bring him here to me.”
WEB.18: Jesus rebuked him, the demon went out of him, and the boy was cured from that hour.
WEB.19: Then the disciples came to Jesus privately, and said, “Why weren’t we able to cast it out?”
WEB.20: He said to them, “Because of your unbelief. For most certainly I tell you, if you have faith as a grain of mustard seed, you will tell this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move; and nothing will be impossible for you.
WEB.21: But this kind doesn’t go out except by prayer and fasting.”
WEB.22: While they were staying in Galilee, Jesus said to them, “The Son of Man is about to be delivered up into the hands of men,
WEB.23: and they will kill him, and the third day he will be raised up.”  They were exceedingly sorry.
WEB.24: When they had come to Capernaum, those who collected the didrachma coins  came to Peter, and said, “Doesn’t your teacher pay the didrachma?”
WEB.25: He said, “Yes.” When he came into the house, Jesus anticipated him, saying, “What do you think, Simon? From whom do the kings of the earth receive toll or tribute? From their children, or from strangers?”
WEB.26: Peter said to him, “From strangers.” Jesus said to him, “Therefore the children are exempt.
WEB.27: But, lest we cause them to stumble, go to the sea, cast a hook, and take up the first fish that comes up. When you have opened its mouth, you will find a stater coin.  Take that, and give it to them for me and you.”

CSB17.1: After six days <sup>ⓓ</sup> Jesus took Peter, James, and his brother John and led them up on a high mountain by themselves. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.2: He was transfigured in front of them, and his face shone like the sun; his clothes became as white as the light. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.3: Suddenly, Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with him.
CSB17.4: Then Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it’s good for us to be here. I will set up <sup>[1]</sup> three shelters here: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.” <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.5: While he was still speaking, suddenly a bright cloud covered <sup>[2]</sup> them, and a voice from the cloud said: “This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well-pleased. Listen to him!” <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.6: When the disciples <sup>ⓘ</sup> heard this, they fell facedown and were terrified.
CSB17.7: Jesus came up, touched them, and said, “Get up; don’t be afraid.” <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.8: When they looked up they saw no one except Jesus alone.
CSB17.9: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, <sup>ⓚ</sup> “Don’t tell anyone about the vision until the Son of Man is raised <sup>[3]</sup>from the dead.”
CSB17.10: So the disciples asked him, “Why then do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.11: “Elijah is coming <sup>[4]</sup> and will restore everything,” he replied. <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.12: “But I tell you: Elijah has already come, and they didn’t recognize him. On the contrary, they did whatever they pleased to him. In the same way the Son of Man is going to suffer at their hands.” <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.13: Then the disciples understood that he had spoken to them about John the Baptist.
CSB17.14: When they reached the crowd, <sup>ⓐ</sup> a man approached and knelt down before him.
CSB17.15: “Lord,” he said, “have mercy on my son, because he has seizures <sup>[6]</sup> and suffers terribly. He often falls into the fire and often into the water.
CSB17.16: I brought him to your disciples, but they couldn’t heal him.” <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.17: Jesus replied, “You unbelieving and perverse generation, how long will I be with you? How long must I put up with you? Bring him here to me.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.18: Then Jesus rebuked the demon, <sup>[7]</sup> and it <sup>[8]</sup> came out of him, <sup>ⓓ</sup> and from that moment <sup>[9]</sup> the boy was healed.
CSB17.19: Then the disciples approached Jesus privately and said, “Why couldn’t we drive it out?”
CSB17.20: “Because of your little faith,” he <sup>[10]</sup> told them. “For truly I tell you, if you have faith the size of  <sup>[11]</sup>a mustard seed, you will tell this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move. <sup>ⓔ</sup>Nothing will be impossible for you.” <sup>[12]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.22: As <sup>ⓖ</sup> they were gathering together <sup>[13]</sup> in Galilee, Jesus told them, “The Son of Man is about to be betrayed into the hands of men.
CSB17.23: They will kill him, and on the third day he will be raised up.” And they were deeply distressed.
CSB17.24: When they came to Capernaum, those who collected the temple tax approached Peter and said, “Doesn’t your teacher pay the temple tax?” <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.25: “Yes,” he said. When he went into the house, Jesus spoke to him first, <sup>[14]</sup> “What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect tariffs or taxes? From their sons or from strangers?” <sup>[15]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.26: “From strangers,” he said. <sup>[16]</sup>  “Then the sons are free,” Jesus told him.
CSB17.27: “But, so we won’t offend them, go to the sea, cast in a fishhook, and take the first fish that you catch. When you open its mouth you’ll find a coin. <sup>[17]</sup>Take it and give it to them for me and you.” <sup>ⓙ</sup>

AMP.1: Six days later Jesus took with Him Peter and James and John the brother of James, and led them up on a high mountain by themselves.
AMP.2: And His appearance changed dramatically in their presence; and His face shone [with heavenly glory, clear and bright] like the sun, and His clothing became as white as light.
AMP.3: And behold, Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Jesus.
AMP.4: Then Peter began to speak and said to Jesus, "Lord, it is good and delightful and auspicious that we are here; if You wish, I will put up three [sacred] tents here—one for You, one for Moses, and one for Elijah."
AMP.5: While he was still speaking, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them, and a voice from the cloud said, " This is My beloved Son, with whom I am well-pleased and delighted! Listen to Him!"
AMP.6: When the disciples heard it, they fell on their faces and were terrified.
AMP.7: But Jesus came and touched them and said, "Get up, and do not be afraid."
AMP.8: And when they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus Himself alone.
AMP.9: And as they were going down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, "Do not tell anyone what you have seen until the Son of Man has been raised from the dead."
AMP.10: The disciples asked Him, "Then why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?"
AMP.11: He answered and said, "Elijah is coming and will restore all things;
AMP.12: but I say to you that Elijah has come already, and they did not recognize him, but did to him as they wished. The Son of Man is also going to suffer at their hands."
AMP.13: Then the disciples understood that He had spoken to them about John the Baptist.
AMP.14: When they approached the crowd, a man came up to Jesus, kneeling before Him and saying,
AMP.15: "Lord, have mercy on my son, for he is a lunatic (moonstruck) and suffers terribly; for he often falls into the fire and often into the water.
AMP.16: And I brought him to Your disciples, and they were not able to heal him."
AMP.17: And Jesus answered, "You unbelieving and perverted generation, how long shall I be with you? How long shall I put up with you? Bring him here to Me."
AMP.18: Jesus rebuked the demon, and it came out of him, and the boy was healed at once.
AMP.19: Then the disciples came to Jesus privately and asked, "Why could we not drive it out?"
AMP.20: He answered, "Because of your little faith [your lack of trust and confidence in the power of God]; for I assure you and most solemnly say to you, if you have [living] faith the size of a mustard seed, you will say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and [if it is God's will] it will move; and nothing will be impossible for you.
AMP.21: [But this kind of demon does not go out except by prayer and fasting.]"
AMP.22: When they were gathering together in Galilee, Jesus said to them, "The Son of Man is going to be betrayed and handed over to men [who are His enemies];
AMP.23: and they will kill Him, and He will be raised [from death to life] on the third day." And they were deeply grieved and distressed.
AMP.24: When they arrived in Capernaum, the collectors of the half-shekel [temple tax] went up to Peter and said, "Does not your teacher pay the half-shekel?"
AMP.25: Peter answered, "Yes." And when he came home, Jesus spoke to him first, saying, "What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly rulers collect duties or taxes, from their sons or from strangers?"
AMP.26: When Peter said, "From strangers," Jesus said to him, "Then the sons are exempt [from taxation].
AMP.27: However, so that we do not offend them, go to the sea and throw in a hook, and take the first fish that comes up; and when you open its mouth, you will find a shekel. Take it and give it to them [to pay the temple tax] for you and Me."

MSG.1: Six days later, three of them saw that glory. Jesus took Peter and the brothers, James and John, and led them up a high mountain.
MSG.2: His appearance changed from the inside out, right before their eyes. Sunlight poured from his face. His clothes were filled with light.
MSG.3: Then they realized that Moses and Elijah were also there in deep conversation with him.
MSG.4: Peter broke in, "Master, this is a great moment! What would you think if I built three memorials here on the mountain—one for you, one for Moses, one for Elijah?"
MSG.5: While he was going on like this, babbling, a light-radiant cloud enveloped them, and sounding from deep in the cloud a voice: "This is my Son, marked by my love, focus of my delight. Listen to him."
MSG.6: When the disciples heard it, they fell flat on their faces, scared to death.
MSG.7: But Jesus came over and touched them. "Don't be afraid."
MSG.8: When they opened their eyes and looked around all they saw was Jesus, only Jesus.
MSG.9: Coming down the mountain, Jesus swore them to secrecy. "Don't breathe a word of what you've seen. After the Son of Man is raised from the dead, you are free to talk."
MSG.10: The disciples, meanwhile, were asking questions. "Why do the religion scholars say that Elijah has to come first?"
MSG.11: Jesus answered, "Elijah does come and get everything ready.
MSG.12: I'm telling you, Elijah has already come but they didn't know him when they saw him. They treated him like dirt, the same way they are about to treat the Son of Man."
MSG.13: That's when the disciples realized that all along he had been talking about John the Baptizer.
MSG.14: At the bottom of the mountain, they were met by a crowd of waiting people. As they approached, a man came out of the crowd and fell to his knees begging,
MSG.15: "Master, have mercy on my son. He goes out of his mind and suffers terribly, falling into seizures. Frequently he is pitched into the fire, other times into the river.
MSG.16: I brought him to your disciples, but they could do nothing for him."
MSG.17: Jesus said, "What a generation! No sense of God! No focus to your lives! How many times do I have to go over these things? How much longer do I have to put up with this? Bring the boy here."
MSG.18: He ordered the afflicting demon out—and it was out, gone. From that moment on the boy was well.
MSG.19: When the disciples had Jesus off to themselves, they asked, "Why couldn't we throw it out?"
MSG.20: "Because you're not yet taking God seriously," said Jesus. "The simple truth is that if you had a mere kernel of faith, a poppy seed, say, you would tell this mountain, 'Move!' and it would move. There is nothing you wouldn't be able to tackle."
MSG.22: As they were regrouping in Galilee, Jesus told them, "The Son of Man is about to be betrayed to some people who want nothing to do with God.
MSG.23: They will murder him—and three days later he will be raised alive." The disciples felt terrible.
MSG.24: When they arrived at Capernaum, the tax men came to Peter and asked, "Does your teacher pay taxes?"
MSG.25: Peter said, "Of course." But as soon as they were in the house, Jesus confronted him. "Simon, what do you think? When a king levies taxes, who pays—his children or his subjects?"
MSG.26: He answered, "His subjects." Jesus said, "Then the children get off free, right?
MSG.27: But so we don't upset them needlessly, go down to the lake, cast a hook, and pull in the first fish that bites. Open its mouth and you'll find a coin. Take it and give it to the tax men. It will be enough for both of us."

NIV.1:
The Transfiguration
After six days Jesus took with him Peter, James and John the brother of James, and led them up a high mountain by themselves.
NIV.2: There he was transfigured before them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as the light.
NIV.3: Just then there appeared before them Moses and Elijah, talking with Jesus.
NIV.4: Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you wish, I will put up three shelters—one for you, one for Moses and one for Elijah.”
NIV.5: While he was still speaking, a bright cloud enveloped them, and a voice from the cloud said, “This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased. Listen to him!”
NIV.6: When the disciples heard this, they fell facedown to the ground, terrified.
NIV.7: But Jesus came and touched them. “Get up,” he said. “Don’t be afraid.”
NIV.8: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
NIV.9: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus instructed them, “Don’t tell anyone what you have seen, until the Son of Man has been raised from the dead.”
NIV.10: The disciples asked him, “Why then do the teachers of the law say that Elijah must come first?”
NIV.11: Jesus replied, “To be sure, Elijah comes and will restore all things.
NIV.12: But I tell you, Elijah has already come, and they did not recognize him, but have done to him everything they wished. In the same way the Son of Man is going to suffer at their hands.”
NIV.13: Then the disciples understood that he was talking to them about John the Baptist.
NIV.14:
The Healing of a Boy With a Demon
When they came to the crowd, a man approached Jesus and knelt before him.
NIV.15: “Lord, have mercy on my son,” he said. “He has seizures and is suffering greatly. He often falls into the fire or into the water.
NIV.16: I brought him to your disciples, but they could not heal him.”
NIV.17: “O unbelieving and perverse generation,” Jesus replied, “how long shall I stay with you? How long shall I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.”
NIV.18: Jesus rebuked the demon, and it came out of the boy, and he was healed from that moment.
NIV.19: Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and asked, “Why couldn’t we drive it out?”
NIV.20: He replied, “Because you have so little faith. I tell you the truth, if you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, ‘Move from here to there’ and it will move. Nothing will be impossible for you.”
NIV.22: When they came together in Galilee, he said to them, “The Son of Man is going to be betrayed into the hands of men.
NIV.23: They will kill him, and on the third day he will be raised to life.” And the disciples were filled with grief.
NIV.24:
The Temple Tax
After Jesus and his disciples arrived in Capernaum, the collectors of the two-drachma tax came to Peter and asked, “Doesn’t your teacher pay the temple tax?”
NIV.25:
“Yes, he does,” he replied.
When Peter came into the house, Jesus was the first to speak. “What do you think, Simon?” he asked. “From whom do the kings of the earth collect duty and taxes—from their own sons or from others?”
NIV.26:
“From others,” Peter answered.
“Then the sons are exempt,” Jesus said to him.
NIV.27: “But so that we may not offend them, go to the lake and throw out your line. Take the first fish you catch; open its mouth and you will find a four-drachma coin. Take it and give it to them for my tax and yours.”

NRSVCE.1: Six days later, Jesus took with him Peter and James and his brother John and led them up a high mountain, by themselves.
NRSVCE.2: And he was transfigured before them, and his face shone like the sun, and his clothes became dazzling white.
NRSVCE.3: Suddenly there appeared to them Moses and Elijah, talking with him.
NRSVCE.4: Then Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here; if you wish, I will make three dwellings here, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
NRSVCE.5: While he was still speaking, suddenly a bright cloud overshadowed them, and from the cloud a voice said, “This is my Son, the Beloved; with him I am well pleased; listen to him!”
NRSVCE.6: When the disciples heard this, they fell to the ground and were overcome by fear.
NRSVCE.7: But Jesus came and touched them, saying, “Get up and do not be afraid.”
NRSVCE.8: And when they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus himself alone.
NRSVCE.9: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus ordered them, “Tell no one about the vision until after the Son of Man has been raised from the dead.”
NRSVCE.10: And the disciples asked him, “Why, then, do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”
NRSVCE.11: He replied, “Elijah is indeed coming and will restore all things;
NRSVCE.12: but I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they did not recognize him, but they did to him whatever they pleased. So also the Son of Man is about to suffer at their hands.”
NRSVCE.13: Then the disciples understood that he was speaking to them about John the Baptist.
NRSVCE.14: When they came to the crowd, a man came to him, knelt before him,
NRSVCE.15: and said, “Lord, have mercy on my son, for he is an epileptic and he suffers terribly; he often falls into the fire and often into the water.
NRSVCE.16: And I brought him to your disciples, but they could not cure him.”
NRSVCE.17: Jesus answered, “You faithless and perverse generation, how much longer must I be with you? How much longer must I put up with you? Bring him here to me.”
NRSVCE.18: And Jesus rebuked the demon, and it came out of him, and the boy was cured instantly.
NRSVCE.19: Then the disciples came to Jesus privately and said, “Why could we not cast it out?”
NRSVCE.20: He said to them, “Because of your little faith. For truly I tell you, if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you will say to this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move; and nothing will be impossible for you.”
NRSVCE.22: As they were gathering in Galilee, Jesus said to them, “The Son of Man is going to be betrayed into human hands,
NRSVCE.23: and they will kill him, and on the third day he will be raised.” And they were greatly distressed.
NRSVCE.24: When they reached Capernaum, the collectors of the temple tax came to Peter and said, “Does your teacher not pay the temple tax?”
NRSVCE.25: He said, “Yes, he does.” And when he came home, Jesus spoke of it first, asking, “What do you think, Simon? From whom do kings of the earth take toll or tribute? From their children or from others?”
NRSVCE.26: When Peter said, “From others,” Jesus said to him, “Then the children are free.
NRSVCE.27: However, so that we do not give offense to them, go to the sea and cast a hook; take the first fish that comes up; and when you open its mouth, you will find a coin; take that and give it to them for you and me.”

NET.1: Six days later Jesus took with him Peter, James, and John the brother of James, and led them privately up a high mountain.
NET.2: And he was transfigured before them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
NET.3: Then Moses and Elijah also appeared before them, talking with him.
NET.4: So Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you want, I will make three shelters — one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
NET.5: While he was still speaking, a bright cloud overshadowed them, and a voice from the cloud said, “This is my one dear Son, in whom I take great delight. Listen to him!”
NET.6: When the disciples heard this, they were overwhelmed with fear and threw themselves down with their faces to the ground.
NET.7: But Jesus came and touched them. “Get up,” he said. “Do not be afraid.”
NET.8: When they looked up, all they saw was Jesus alone.
NET.9: As they were coming down from the mountain, Jesus commanded them, “Do not tell anyone about the vision until the Son of Man is raised from the dead.”
NET.10: The disciples asked him, “Why then do the experts in the law say that Elijah must come first?”
NET.11: He answered, “Elijah does indeed come first and will restore all things.
NET.12: And I tell you that Elijah has already come. Yet they did not recognize him, but did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way, the Son of Man will suffer at their hands.”
NET.13: Then the disciples understood that he was speaking to them about John the Baptist.
NET.14: When they came to the crowd, a man came to him, knelt before him,
NET.15: and said, “Lord, have mercy on my son, because he has seizures and suffers terribly, for he often falls into the fire and into the water.
NET.16: I brought him to your disciples, but they were not able to heal him.”
NET.17: Jesus answered, “You unbelieving and perverse generation! How much longer must I be with you? How much longer must I endure you? Bring him here to me.”
NET.18: Then Jesus rebuked the demon and it came out of him, and the boy was healed from that moment.
NET.19: Then the disciples came to Jesus privately and said, “Why couldn’t we cast it out?”
NET.20: He told them, “It was because of your little faith. I tell you the truth, if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you will say to this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move; nothing will be impossible for you.”
NET.22: When they gathered together in Galilee, Jesus told them, “The Son of Man is going to be betrayed into the hands of men.
NET.23: They will kill him, and on the third day he will be raised.” And they became greatly distressed.
NET.24: After they arrived in Capernaum, the collectors of the temple tax came to Peter and said, “Your teacher pays the double drachma tax, doesn’t he?”
NET.25: He said, “Yes.” When Peter came into the house, Jesus spoke to him first, “What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect tolls or taxes — from their sons or from foreigners?”
NET.26: After he said, “From foreigners,” Jesus said to him, “Then the sons are free.
NET.27: But so that we don’t offend them, go to the lake and throw out a hook. Take the first fish that comes up, and when you open its mouth, you will find a four drachma coin. Take that and give it to them for me and you.”
